label: Iranian forces strike Qatar and UAE LNG infrastructure causing energy disruptions
label_zh: 伊朗部隊襲擊卡塔爾及阿聯酋液化天然氣設施 釀能源供應中斷

timeline.title: Iranian Missile Attacks Devastate Qatari LNG Hub and UAE Gas Infrastructure in Regional Escalation
timeline.title_zh: 導彈襲擊伊朗摧毀卡塔爾液化天然氣樞紐及阿聯酋燃氣基礎設施，地區局勢升級
timeline.sub_title: Tehran’s strikes on Ras Laffan and Habshan follow US-Israeli attacks on Iranian fields, knocking out 17% of Qatar’s export capacity and triggering a global surge in energy prices.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 德黑蘭襲擊拉斯拉凡和哈卜尚，繼美以軍襲擊伊朗油田後，導致卡塔爾出口能力損失17%，並觸發全球能源價格飆升。
timeline.summary:
In a dramatic escalation of the multi-week conflict in the Middle East, Iranian forces launched a coordinated wave of missile and drone strikes against critical energy infrastructure in Qatar and the United Arab Emirates. The attacks, occurring between late Wednesday and early Thursday, primarily targeted the Ras Laffan Industrial City in Qatar and the Habshan gas complex in the UAE. Qatar’s state-owned energy giant, QatarEnergy, confirmed that the strikes caused extensive damage to two of its fourteen LNG trains and a gas-to-liquids facility. These [ref:2] disruptions have effectively knocked out approximately 17 percent of Qatar’s total liquefied natural gas export capacity, a blow that officials estimate could take three to five years to fully repair and cost upwards of $20 billion in annual revenue.

In the United Arab Emirates, authorities were forced to shut down major gas processing operations at the Habshan facility and the Bab oil field following the interception of missiles and the resulting impact of falling debris. While no casualties were reported in either Qatar or the UAE, the environmental and economic damage is severe. Qatar responded to the aggression by declaring Iranian military and security attachés persona non grata, giving them 24 hours to leave the country. This diplomatic [ref:1, 7, 6] rupture highlights the crumbling trust between Tehran and its Gulf neighbors, who had previously sought to maintain neutrality in the ongoing war between Iran and the U.S.-Israeli coalition.

The strikes are widely viewed as a direct retaliation for an Israeli-led operation on March 18 that targeted Iran’s South Pars gas field at Asaluyeh. Iranian state media had issued explicit warnings just hours prior to the strikes, urging personnel to evacuate petrochemical sites across Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar. This "energy-for-energy" doctrine has now moved the conflict beyond traditional military targets and into a phase of total economic warfare. The IRGC has signaled that any further attacks on Iranian soil will result in the complete destruction of regional energy hubs, further endangering the world's most critical fuel supply routes.

Global energy markets reacted with immediate volatility. Brent crude oil prices briefly surged above $119 per barrel, a 60 percent increase since the start of the conflict in late February, while European natural gas benchmarks spiked by more than 35 percent. Analysts warn of a looming "doomsday scenario" for global energy security, as the damage to Ras Laffan threatens long-term supply contracts with major economies including China, South Korea, and several European nations. The effective closure [ref:5] of the Strait of Hormuz has further exacerbated the crisis, leaving land-based pipelines and Red Sea ports as the only remaining, yet vulnerable, outlets for regional exports.

International political fallout has been swift, with U.S. President Donald Trump issuing a stern warning to Tehran on social media. Trump stated that while he would restrain further Israeli strikes on Iranian gas fields for the moment, any subsequent attacks on Qatari infrastructure would result in the U.S. "massively blowing up" the entirety of the South Pars field. Meanwhile, a coalition of [ref:3] Gulf states has requested an urgent debate at the United Nations Human Rights Council, characterizing the strikes on vital civilian infrastructure as a grave threat to international peace. As fire crews in Qatar and the UAE work to secure damaged sites, the region remains on a knife-edge, awaiting the next move in a rapidly widening war.
timeline.summary_zh:
在中東持續數週衝突的戲劇性升級中，伊朗部隊對卡塔爾和阿拉伯聯合酋長國的關鍵能源基礎設施發動了協調一致的導彈和無人機襲擊。襲擊發生在週三晚至週四凌晨，主要針對卡塔爾的拉斯拉凡工業城和阿聯酋的哈卜尚燃氣綜合設施。卡塔爾國營能源巨頭 QatarEnergy 確認，襲擊對其十四個液化天然氣（LNG）生產線中的兩個以及一個氣轉液設施造成了廣泛損壞。這些中斷實際上摧毀了卡塔爾約17%的總液化天然氣出口能力，官員估計完全修復此損壞可能需要三到五年，並導致每年高達200億美元的收入損失。[ref:2]

在阿拉伯聯合酋長國，當局在導彈被攔截以及隨後墜落碎片造成的影響後，被迫關閉了哈卜尚設施和巴布油田的主要燃氣處理業務。儘管卡塔爾和阿聯酋均無人員傷亡報告，但環境和經濟損害嚴重。卡塔爾對這次侵略的反應是宣布伊朗軍事和安全參贊為不受歡迎的人（persona non grata），給予他們24小時內離開該國。這種外交破裂凸顯了德黑蘭與其海灣鄰國之間日益喪失的信任，這些鄰國此前曾試圖在伊朗與美以聯軍之間持續的戰爭中保持中立。[ref:1, 7, 6]

此次襲擊被廣泛認為是對以色列於3月18日針對伊朗南帕斯（South Pars）天然氣田發動的襲擊的直接報復。伊朗國家媒體在襲擊前幾小時發出了明確警告，敦促人員撤離沙特阿拉伯、阿聯酋和卡塔爾的石化網站。這一「能源換能源」的理論已將衝突從傳統軍事目標擴展到全面的經濟戰階段。伊斯蘭革命衛隊（IRGC）已表明，任何對伊朗本土的進一步襲擊都將導致該地區能源中心的徹底毀滅，進一步危及全球最關鍵的燃料供應路線。

全球能源市場立即作出反應，出現劇烈波動。布蘭特原油價格一度飆升至每桶119美元以上，自2月底衝突開始以來上漲了60%，而歐洲天然氣基準價格則上漲超過35%。分析師警告全球能源安全可能面臨「末日情景」，拉斯拉凡的損壞威脅著與中國、韓國和幾個歐洲國家等主要經濟體簽署的長期供應合約。霍爾木茲海峽的有效關閉進一步加劇了危機，僅剩下陸上管道和紅海港口作為該地區出口的剩餘但脆弱的通道。[ref:5]

國際政治影響迅速顯現，美國總統特朗普在社交媒體上發布了對德黑蘭的嚴厲警告。特朗普表示，儘管他目前會約束以色列進一步襲擊伊朗的天然氣田，但任何隨後對卡塔爾基礎設施的襲擊將導致美國「大規模摧毀」整個南帕斯油田。同時，一個海灣國家聯盟已要求在聯合國人權理事會召開緊急辯論，將襲擊關鍵民用基礎設施的行為定性為對國際和平的嚴重威脅。隨著卡塔爾和阿聯酋的消防隊努力保護受損現場，該地區仍處於緊張狀態，等待著這場迅速擴大的戰爭的下一步行動。[ref:3]
